Uses of Class
org.jscience.social.economics.Worker

Packages that use Worker
Package
Description
org.jscience.social.arts
- artworks, music, literature classification.
org.jscience.social.economics
Provides comprehensive mathematical models for economic systems, agent-based modeling, and financial analysis.
org.jscience.social.law
Provides the org.jscience.social.law module.
org.jscience.social.medicine.consultation
Core functionality for the org.jscience.social.medicine.consultation package in the JScience library.

    Artist
    Represents an artist (painter, sculptor, musician, etc.) as a specialized social role.

    Method
    Description
    static Worker
    Worker.getCommonRoot(Worker w1, Worker w2)
    Finds the lowest common supervisor for two workers.
    Methods in org.jscience.social.economics that return types with arguments of type Worker
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static Tree<Worker>
    Worker.extractTree(Worker w1, Worker w2)
    Creates a tree structure representing the hierarchy between two workers.
    Set<Worker>
    Worker.getAllSubalterns()
    Returns all subordinates at any depth.
    Set<Worker>
    Organigram.getAllWorkers()
    Returns all workers in the hierarchy.
    Set<Worker>
    Worker.getChiefs()
    Returns an unmodifiable set of immediate supervisors.
    Set<Worker>
    Worker.getCoWorkers()
    Returns workers having the same immediate supervisors.
    Set<Worker>
    Worker.getLeaders()
    Returns the set of workers at the top of the hierarchy for this worker.
    static List<Worker>
    Worker.getLineage(Worker root, Worker target)
    Returns the workers path from a root to a target worker.
    Set<Worker>
    Worker.getSubalterns()
    Returns an unmodifiable set of immediate subordinates.
    Set<Worker>
    Worker.getSubalternsAtLevelK(int k)
    Returns all subordinates at a specific depth level.
    Set<Worker>
    Factory.getWorkers()
    Returns the workers associated with this factory.
    Set<Worker>
    Organigram.getWorkers()
    Returns the workers associated with this organigram.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    Worker.addSubaltern(Worker child)
    Adds an immediate subordinate.
    void
    Factory.addWorker(Worker worker)
    Adds a worker to the organization.
    void
    Organigram.addWorker(Worker worker)
    Adds a worker to the organigram.
    static Tree<Worker>

    Creates a tree structure representing the hierarchy between two workers.
    static Worker
    Worker.getCommonRoot(Worker w1, Worker w2)
    Finds the lowest common supervisor for two workers.
    static List<Worker>
    Worker.getLineage(Worker root, Worker target)
    Returns the workers path from a root to a target worker.
    boolean
    Worker.hasChild(Worker child)
    Checks if a specific worker is an immediate subordinate.
    boolean
    Worker.hasDistantSubaltern(Worker target)
    Checks if a worker is a subordinate at any depth.
    void
    Worker.removeSubaltern(Worker child)
    Removes an immediate subordinate.
    void
    Worker.reparent(Worker oldParent, Worker newParent)
    Change a supervisor for this worker.

  • Uses of Worker in org.jscience.social.law

    Subclasses of Worker in org.jscience.social.law
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Judge
    Represents a judge responsible for presiding over legal proceedings and trials.
    class 
    JuryMember
    Represents a member of a jury whose role is to decide if the defendant is guilty or liable, or to determine fact-based outcomes in a trial.
    class 
    Lawyer
    The Lawyer class represents a legal professional whose primary role is to provide legal advice and represent individuals or organizations in legal matters.
    class 
    Prosecutor
    Represents a prosecutor, who is a legal professional responsible for presenting the case in a criminal trial against an individual accused of breaking the law.

    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Doctor
    The Doctor class provides some useful information for people whose job is to cure individuals.
